settings.heading: Ingredients
settings.text: A curated collection of clinical white papers and research insights exploring magnesium’s impact on human physiology.

settings.title: Magnesium Sulphate
settings.full_desc:
• Also known as Epsom salt. It is the main magnesium base used in warm soak and topical reset rituals.
  • Best known on Bubble Me for body unwind, muscle ease, and head-comfort routines.

settings.title: Ylang Ylang Oil
settings.full_desc:
• Often used in unwind rituals where the goal is to soften stress and tension.
  • Works well when paired with a soak format for end-of-day reset.

settings.title: Magnesium Bisglycinate
settings.full_desc:
• A widely used oral magnesium form often chosen for daily support and repeat use.
  • Works best on this page as part of a calm, sleep, and routine-support conversation.

settings.title: Himalayan Pink Salt
settings.full_desc:
• Used as a mineral-rich salt base in Bubble Me soak rituals.
  • Helps make the soak feel more complete, grounding, and skin-friendly alongside

settings.title: Chamomile
settings.full_desc:
• A familiar calming botanical often linked with evening support and softer wind-down rituals.
  • Helps make the routine feel more settled, especially in sleep-led formats.

settings.title: Lavender
settings.full_desc:
• Known for its soothing aroma and strong bedtime association.
  • Often used where the goal is to make the body and mind feel ready to slow down.

settings.title: Magnesium L-Threonate
settings.full_desc:
• Often discussed in brain, focus, and mental-clarity conversations.
  • Best explained simply here, then explored deeper on the full ingredient page if needed.

settings.title: Peppermint
settings.full_desc:
• Known for its cooling, fresh sensory feel.
  • Commonly used in head-comfort and quick-reset rituals, especially when the body feels tense or overloaded.

settings.title: Magnesium Taurate
settings.full_desc:
• Used in daily magnesium blends to make the support story feel more complete, not one-dimensional.
  • Helpful on this page as part of a “steady daily support” discussion.

settings.title: Jasmine Oil
settings.full_desc:
• Adds a soft floral cue that supports slowing down after a mentally heavy day.
  • Better for this page as an emotional-unwind ingredient than a hard “function” claim.

settings.title: Blue Corn Flower
settings.full_desc:
• Used more for ritual feel, sensory softness, and blend identity than for a main magnesium role.
  • Helps stress and mood-reset blends feel gentler and more complete.

settings.title: Spearmint
settings.full_desc:
• Brings a lighter cooling feel that works well in on-the-go head support rituals.
  • Best used on this page as part of the sensory comfort story, not as a dramatic claim.

settings.title: Eucalyptus
settings.full_desc:
• Adds a sharper, clearer aroma that fits head-comfort and reset rituals well.
  • Useful in formulas meant for heavy-head, tired-body, or post-day recovery moments.

settings.title: Rosemary
settings.full_desc:
• Used for a fresh, uplifting sensory layer in head-comfort routines.
  • Works best here as part of the experience and feel of the ritual.

settings.title: Tea Tree Oil
settings.full_desc:
• Used in recovery-focused blends for a crisp, spa-like reset feel.
  • Fits body-pain and post-workout rituals more naturally than sleep-led routines.

settings.title: Safflower
settings.full_desc:
• Helps round out body-reset blends in a softer, more ritual-led way.
  • Best treated here as a supporting ingredient, not the hero of the formula.

settings.title: Rosa Centifolia
settings.full_desc:
• Used to give soak rituals a softer aroma and a more soothing feel on the skin.
  • Helps blends feel gentler and more restorative, especially in unwind rituals.

settings.title: Citrus Limon
settings.full_desc:
• Adds a bright, clean note that makes the soak feel fresher and lighter.
  • Useful more as a sensory support ingredient than a lead concern ingredient.

settings.title: Arrowroot
settings.full_desc:
• Helps the soak blend feel smoother and more balanced as a formula.
  • This is a support ingredient, so it should be explained briefly and simply.

settings.title: Sesame Oil
settings.full_desc:
• Used as a nourishing base in balm formulas so the product glides better on skin.
  • Supports the feel and use experience of the ritual more than the magnesium story itself.

settings.title: Form decides the role
settings.description:
The same magnesium story can show up in different ways.

A soak is different from a balm.

A daily tablet is different from both.
settings.caption: Chelated molecules act as a 'trojan horse' moving effortlessly through the intestinal wall.

settings.title: Chelated & Complexed
settings.description: Magnesium bonded to amino acids mimics the way nutrients are found in nature, facilitating superior transport.
